Who is the fastest run scorer in the Test Match ?
explanation:
To determine the fastest run scorer in the Test Match, we need to calculate the strike rate of each player in both innings. The strike rate is defined as the number of runs scored per 100 balls faced. The formula for the strike rate can be expressed as:

$$ \text{Strike Rate} = \left( \frac{\text{Runs Scored}}{\text{Balls Faced}} \right) \times 100 $$

Let's calculate the strike rate for each player in both innings:

Player A:

First Innings: $$ \text{SR}_1 = \left( \frac{61}{99} \right) \times 100 = 61.62 $$

Second Innings: $$ \text{SR}_2 = \left( \frac{14}{76} \right) \times 100 = 18.42 $$

Player B:

First Innings: $$ \text{SR}_1 = \left( \frac{05}{12} \right) \times 100 = 41.67 $$

Second Innings: $$ \text{SR}_2 = \left( \frac{50}{85} \right) \times 100 = 58.82 $$

Player C:

First Innings: $$ \text{SR}_1 = \left( \frac{15}{75} \right) \times 100 = 20.00 $$

Second Innings: $$ \text{SR}_2 = \left( \frac{20}{50} \right) \times 100 = 40.00 $$

Player D:

First Innings: $$ \text{SR}_1 = \left( \frac{13}{55} \right) \times 100 = 23.64 $$

Second Innings: $$ \text{SR}_2 = \left( \frac{12}{50} \right) \times 100 = 24.00 $$

Now, we determine the average strike rate for each player across both innings:

Player A: $$ \text{SR}_{avg} = \frac{61.62 + 18.42}{2} = 40.02 $$

Player B: $$ \text{SR}_{avg} = \frac{41.67 + 58.82}{2} = 50.25 $$

Player C: $$ \text{SR}_{avg} = \frac{20.00 + 40.00}{2} = 30.00 $$

Player D: $$ \text{SR}_{avg} = \frac{23.64 + 24.00}{2} = 23.82 $$

The highest average strike rate is 50.25, which belongs to Player B. Therefore, the fastest run scorer in the Test Match is:

Option B: B
